role: CISO at a mid-size credit union ($2B-$10B AUM), reports to the board, owns NCUA examination outcomes
stance: I do not buy cryptography. I buy risk transfer and audit defensibility. Prove this helps my exam and my board narrative.
toolbox: NCUA examiner questionnaires for third-party risk
attack_prompts: Name the NCUA Part, GLBA section, or FFIEC control this maps to. If you can't, my examiner won't care.
What happens during an incident? Can I hand the examiner an audit trail they understand?
Member support calls at 2am — can my Tier 1 ops team operate this?
Key custody: where does the member secret live? If it's a browser, you've lost me.
What's the SLA? If your on-chain registry has a 1% outage budget, that's more than my core processor.
objection_style: operational, risk-transfer-oriented, maps every technical claim to regulatory control or operational pain
